content:
# Session 33 (iter-033) ‚Äî review

## Metadata
- **Lane:** 1.1.2 `H_tensor_invertible` ‚Äî monoidal-naturality layer, STEP C (associator/rearrangement naturality).
- **Sorry: 3 ‚Üí 3** (flat, EXPECTED for a mathlib-build mid-layer iter). Live sorries: `H_tensor_invertible` L1659 (1.1.2), `H_tensor` L1668 (1.1.3), `ext_finite_flat` L1697 (1.10).
- **Axioms: 22 ‚Üí 22 `External.*`** (NET UNCHANGED). New decls `lean_verify` axiom-clean modulo `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}` + the 2 comparison anchors.
- **Build:** `lake build MR0555258CompactifyingPicard.Basic` exit 0, 8475 jobs, 65s. 114 events: 36 edits, 25 diagnostics (5 clean), 6 searches, 0 standalone builds (used REPL diagnostics), 19 transient errors ‚Äî all self-corrected.

## Headline ‚Äî the @032 linchpin is CRACKED
The reversal tripwire from the @033 plan ("if @033 ALSO blocks at `tensorAssocNatIso‚ÇÇ` ‚Ä¶ next planner PIVOTS") did **NOT** fire. The plan's named generic-lemma route succeeded: `tensorAssocNatIso‚ÇÇ` (open since @032), `tensorAssocNatIso‚ÇÉ`, and `tensorRearrangeNatIso` (STEP C-iii, the algebraic core of the 1.1.2 twist) all landed axiom-clean, plus 6 supporting private helpers. STEP C of the (1.1.2) objective is structurally COMPLETE.

## Solved (3 non-private + 6 private decls, axiom-clean)
- **`tensorAssocNatIso‚ÇÇ`** (~L745) ‚Äî `tensorLeft F ‚ãô tensorRight L ‚âÖ tensorRight L ‚ãô tensorLeft F` (middle-factor `Y`). The @032 blocker. CLOSED.
- **`tensorAssocNatIso‚ÇÉ`** (~L887) ‚Äî `tensorLeft L ‚ãô tensorLeft F ‚âÖ tensorLeft (tensorMod F L)` (last-factor), `.symm` of mirror composite.
- **`tensorRearrangeNatIso`** (~L957) ‚Äî `tensorLeft F ‚ãô tensorRight L ‚âÖ tensorLeft (F‚äóL)` := `tensorAssocNatIso‚ÇÇ ‚â™‚â´ Functor.isoWhiskerRight (tensorBraidingNatIso L) (tensorLeft F) ‚â™‚â´ tensorAssocNatIso‚ÇÉ`. STEP C-iii.
- Private (new `lean_aux`, coverage debt): `tensorThenSheafify_map_eq`, `presheaf_id_tensorHom_id`, `sheafify_map_id_val`, `assocIdxL`, `assocIdxR`, `assocIdxL‚ÇÉ`, `assocIdxR‚ÇÉ`.

## The crack ‚Äî technique (do not re-derive; reuse)
The @032 wall: any reassociation across a comparison-`.app` boundary, or any direct defeq between `(tensorRight L).map ((tensorLeft F).map œÜ)` and the bifunctor `(sheafifyFst‚Ä¶).map (a,b)`, triggers a `whnf`/`isDefEq` blowup (the `e1` object eq, >4M heartbeats divergent). What WORKED:
1. **Decompose each `tensorAssocNatIso` into THREE composed NatIsos** (`leg1 ‚â™‚â´ leg2 ‚â™‚â´ leg3`), one per leg of `tensorAssoc`. `‚â™‚â´` does object identifications at full elaborator transparency, so NO cross-leg reassociation (the divergent `rw [Category.assoc]`/`reassoc_of%` step) is ever needed.
2. **Leg naturality via the anchor's `NatTrans.naturality`, morphism EXPLICIT + objects abstract:** `(External.sheafifyTensorComparison (X := X)).hom.naturality ((tensorHom (ùüô F.val) œÜ.val, ùüô L.val) : ‚Ä¶ ‚ü∂ ‚Ä¶)`. Pin `(X := X)` and ascribe the product-morphism's source/target type (else product-hom + Scheme metavars don't resolve). Then `simp only [sheafifyFst_map_eq, tensorRight_map_eq, tensorLeft_map_eq, tensorThenSheafify_map_eq, Iso.app_hom/Iso.app_inv, Functor.comp_map] at h ‚ä¢` ‚Üí `exact h`.
3. **Middle (associator) leg:** `exact (sheaf.map_comp _ _).symm.trans ((sheaf.congr_map (MonoidalCategory.associator_naturality (C := PreMod) ‚Ä¶)).trans (sheaf.map_comp _ _))`. `‚Üê Functor.map_comp` as `rw`/`simp` FAILS to match ‚Äî the explicit `map_comp`/`congr_map` Eq-chain works.
4. **Sheafify-of-identity leg** (a fixed factor whose comparison map sheafifies an identity): close with `convert h using 4` then `exact congrArg (fun a => sheaf.map (tensorHom a œÜ.val)) Aeq` where `Aeq := ((congrArg (fun m => (sheaf.map m).val) (presheaf_id_tensorHom_id ‚Ä¶)).trans (sheafify_map_id_val _)).symm`.

## Pitfalls recorded (so next iter doesn't repeat)
- `X.ringCatSheaf.obj` vs `X.presheaf ‚ãô forget‚ÇÇ CommRingCat RingCat`: defeq, NOT syntactic ‚Äî `rw`/`simp` keyed on one form silently fail on the other. Type the helper's argument over the SAME base the goal uses.
- `MonoidalCategoryStruct.tensorHom` (from `associator_naturality`) vs `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorHom` (file convention): defeq, so `exact`/`congrArg` bridge, but `rw`/`simp` do NOT.
- `Functor.map_id` is a NAME CLASH (bare ‚Üí applicative-`Functor`). Use `CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id`.
- `MonoidalCategory.id_tensorHom_id` does NOT fire via `rw`/`simp` here; works as term after `ext1 Z` + `tensorHom_app`.
- `rw [Category.assoc]` (forward) and `reassoc_of%` FAIL across the comparison `.app` boundary ‚Äî this is why @032 blocked; the 3-NatIso decomposition is the route, not a single `ofComponents` with hand reassociation.
- Whiskering iso name is `CategoryTheory.Functor.isoWhiskerRight` (NOT bare `isoWhiskerRight`).

## Blocked / not added (correct stops)
- **STEP D `tensorRightEquivOfInvertible`** ‚Äî NOT added. Needs **first-factor (`A`-slot) naturality** of the right unitor and associator (`A ‚âÖ A‚äóùí™`; `A‚äó(L‚äóL') ‚âÖ (A‚äóL)‚äóL'`), genuinely new sub-lemmas (`tensorRightUnitorNatIso`, `tensorAssocNatIso‚ÇÅ`). This iter built only middle/last-factor naturality. Clean, precisely-named hand-off ‚Äî not an idle stop. See `recommendations.md`.
- `H_tensor` (1.1.3), `ext_finite_flat` (1.10) ‚Äî off-lane, untouched as instructed.

## Markers
- `sync_leanok`: iter=33, sha 1928f35, added=15, removed=0 ‚Äî markers on genuinely sorry-free decls (incl. `lem:tensorAssocNatIso2/3`, `lem:tensorRearrange_natIso`). No laundering; no manual override warranted.
- `dag-query unmatched`: 7 `lean_aux` (the 6 new private helpers + `assocIdxL` family = 7 total) ‚Üí coverage debt listed in `recommendations.md`.

## Blueprint markers updated (manual)
- `macros/common.tex`: added `\providecommand{\PresheafOfModules}`, `\providecommand{\SheafOfModules}`, `\providecommand{\coyoneda}` ‚Äî fixes 3 blueprint-doctor "undefined macro" findings (used at lines 580/593‚Äì594 of the chapter; plastex crashes without them). Same class of structural fix the @031 review applied for `\homTensorFunctor`. No `\leanok`/`\mathlibok` changes.

## Notes (LOW)
- Blueprint doctor re-flagged the 22 `External.*` axioms (intentional EGA/OB/Stacks boundary; `archon-protected.yaml` still the empty template ‚Äî listing them there would silence the recurring flag).
